Security

Strict Mode
When enabled, enforces settings that prevent the agent from autonomously running targeted exploits and requires human review for all agent actions. Visit antigravity.google/docs/strict-mode for details.

Artifact

Review Policy
Specifies Agent's behavior when asking for review on artifacts, which are documents it creates to enable a richer conversation experience. • Always Proceeds - Agent never asks for review. This maximizes the autonomy of the Agent, but also has the highest risk of the Agent operating over unsafe or injected Artifact content. • Agent Decides - Agent will decide when to ask for review based on task complexity and user preference. • Asks for Review - Agent always asks for review.

Terminal

Terminal Command Auto Execution
• Always Proceed - Agent never asks for confirmation before executing terminal commands (except those in the Deny list). This provides the Agent with the maximum ability to operate over long periods without intervention, but also has the highest risk of an Agent executing an unsafe terminal command. • Request Review - Agent always asks for confirmation before executing terminal commands (except those in the Allow list). Note: A change to this setting will only apply to new messages sent to Agent. In-progress responses will use the previous setting value.
⚠️ Warning: "Always Proceed" is enabled without sandbox protection. This is very dangerous and we do not recommend doing this.
Enable Terminal Sandbox
When enabled, terminal commands run with sandbox restrictions.
Enable Shell Integration
When enabled, Agent will use IDE's shell integration to detect and report terminal command execution. When disabled, the agent will use its own shell. Restart the application for this to take effect.
Allow List Terminal Commands
Agent auto-executes commands matched by an allow list entry. For Unix shells, an allow list entry matches a command if its space-separated tokens form a prefix of the command's tokens. For PowerShell, the entry tokens may match any contiguous subsequence of the command tokens.
Deny List Terminal Commands
Agent asks for permission before executing commands matched by a deny list entry. The deny list follows the same matching rules as the allow list and takes precedence over the allow list.

File Access

Agent Non-Workspace File Access
Allow Agent to view and edit files outside of the current workspace automatically. Use with caution: this provides the Agent access to additional potentially-relevant information, but also allows the Agent to access credential files, secrets, and other files outside of the workspace that could be targeted in prompt injection attacks or other exploits by malicious actors.
Auto-Open Edited Files
Open files in the background if Agent creates or edits them

Automation

Agent Auto-Fix Lints
When enabled, Agent is given awareness of lint errors created by its edits and may fix them without explicit user prompting.

History

Conversation History
When enabled, the agent will be able to access past conversations to inform its responses.
Knowledge
When enabled, the agent will be able to access its knowledge base to inform its responses and automatically generate knowledge items in the background. Disabling this will prevent the agent from accessing existing knowledge items, but will not delete them.

General

Explain and Fix in Current Conversation
When enabled, "Explain and Fix" actions will continue in the current conversation instead of starting a new one.
Open Agent on Reload
Open Agent panel on window reload
Enable Sounds for Agent
When enabled, Antigravity will play a sound when Agent finishes generating a response.
Auto-Expand Changes Overview
When enabled, the Changes Overview toolbar will automatically expand when Agent finishes generating a response.
[Dev] GCP Project ID
GCP Project ID for enterprise features.

General

Enable Browser Tools
When enabled, Agent can use browser tools to open URLs, read web pages, and interact with browser content. This allows the Agent access to important (and often critical) knowledge and methods of validation, but any browser integration does increase exposure to external malicious parties for security exploits.
Browser Javascript Execution Policy
• Disabled - Agent will never run Javascript code in the browser. • Request Review - Agent will always stop to ask for permission to run Javascript code in the browser. • Always Proceed - Agent will not stop to ask for permission to run Javascript in the browser. This provides the Agent with maximum autonomy to perform complex actions and validation in the browser, but also has the highest exposure to security exploits.

Allowlist

Browser URL Allowlist
Control which URLs the browser can access. Add domains or full URLs to the allowlist.

Stitchopen_in_new
The Stitch MCP server enables AI assistants to interact with Stitch for vibe design: generating UI designs from text and images, and accessing project and screen details. See https://stitch.withgoogle.com/docs for more details.
Chrome DevTools MCPopen_in_new
Enable Antigravity to control and inspect a live Chrome browser, with access to the full power of Chrome DevTools for reliable automation, in-depth debugging, and performance analysis.
Google Developer Knowledgeopen_in_new
The Google Developer Knowledge MCP server gives AI-powered development tools the ability to search Google's official developer documentation and retrieve information for Google's products such as Firebase, Google Cloud, Android, Maps, and more. By connecting your AI application straight to our official library of documentation, it ensures the code and guidance you receive are up-to-date and based on authoritative context.
ClickHouseopen_in_new
The ClickHouse MCP server enables agents to securely interact with ClickHouse databases. It provides a universal interface to execute SQL, explore data, and view backup & billing details, allowing agentic tooling to leverage ClickHouse's high-performance analytical capabilities.
Google Compute Engineopen_in_new
The Compute Engine remote MCP server provides a comprehensive set of capabilities that let LLM agents perform a range of infrastructure management tasks including the following: manage virtual machine (VM) instances, manage instance group managers and instance templates, manage disks and snapshots, retrieve information about reservations and commitments.
Google Android Management APIopen_in_new
The Android Management API remote MCP server enables AI agents to programmatically access enterprise mobility data. It allows for natural language queries about device fleets, automated auditing of policy compliance, and the integration of device management data into broader automated workflows.
Google Cloud Resource Manageropen_in_new
The Google Cloud Resource Manager remote MCP server allows you to search your Google Cloud projects from your AI application.
Google Vertex AI Searchopen_in_new
The Vertex AI Search remote MCP server allows you to perform searches on ingested data in Google owned data stores.
Google Cloud Firestoreopen_in_new
The Firestore remote MCP server lets you interact with documents stored in a Firestore database from your AI application.
Google Cloud Loggingopen_in_new
The Cloud Logging remote MCP server provides read access to various resources in the Cloud Logging platform.
Google Managed Service for Apache Kafkaopen_in_new
The Managed Service for Apache Kafka remote MCP server lets you manage Managed Service for Apache Kafka clusters, as well as Kafka Connect clusters.
Google Cloud Monitoringopen_in_new
The Cloud Monitoring remote MCP server provides read access to various resources in the Cloud Monitoring platform.
Google Cloud Pub/Subopen_in_new
The Pub/Sub remote MCP server lets you manage Pub/Sub resources and publish messages. You can create, list, get, update, and delete Pub/Sub topics, subscriptions, and snapshots, as well as publish messages to topics.

### 🏛️ The "God Build" Master Roadmap

#### 1. UI/UX & Aesthetics (The Pixel-Perfect Layer)
*   **Aesthetic Standard**: High-fidelity glassmorphism, dark-mode optimization, and premium typography (Inter/Roboto).
*   **YouTube Topbar (The "6 Things")**:
    *   A pill-shaped, translucent control group injected into the YouTube masthead (to the left of the "Create" button).
    *   **The Icons**: Signal (AI Sort), Notifications (Hoardr Inbox), Statistics (Overlay Toggle), Catch Up (Rocket), Vault (Archive), and Trash (Clear Session).
*   **Nexus Floater**:
    *   **Normal Click (YouTube)**: Toggles the "old" in-page statistics overlay (no popups).
    *   **Normal Click (Other Sites)**: Opens the SocialHoardr Vault.
    *   **Modifier Click (Opt/Alt)**: Opens the advanced "Nexus Menu" for global orchestration.
*   **Notification Panel**: Floating, glassmorphic inbox with smooth scrolling and high contrast for unread alerts.

#### 2. YouTube Stabilization (Content & Analytics)
*   **Smart Hoarding**: Automated capture of video duration, channel names, and timestamps (fixing previous duration parsing inaccuracies).
*   **One-Click Transcripts**: Command to auto-open the YouTube transcript panel, extract text, and save it to the Vault with a high-fidelity visual preview.
*   **Signal Mode**: A persistent state that filters recommendations based on "Learning" vs. "Distraction" metadata (Nexus Intelligence).
*   **Ad-Dodge Analytics**: Tracking and visualizing total ads avoided and time saved.

#### 3. Nexus Intelligence & Vault
*   **Hoarding Density**: A 90-day pulse heatmap in the Vault visualizing activity across all platforms (X, IG, Reddit, YT).
*   **Signal Intelligence (Audit)**: AI-driven categorization of hoarded content to audit attention patterns (identifying momentum-heavy content vs. filler).
*   **Unified Search**: Global search across all captured texts, transcripts, and notifications with platform and date filters.
*   **Data Export**: Filter-respecting CSV/JSON exports that actually match the visible UI view (fixing the "exporting everything" bug).

#### 4. Cross-Platform Coverage
*   **X (Twitter)**: Stealth hoarding of tweets, including "Suggested" content tracking and spam detection.
*   **Instagram**: Capturing Reels, Highlights, and Profile state.
*   **Twitch/Reddit**: Live sighting capture and subreddit density tracking.
*   **Gmail**: Review mode to summarize inbox state.

#### 5. Infrastructure & Resilience
*   **Nexus Webhook**: Real-time automation layer that sends hoarded signals to your global hub (Gravity Hub).
*   **Resilient Injection**: Script stability that survives YouTube's Polymer DOM updates via a multi-selector fallback strategy.
*   **Clean Messaging**: Simplified communication between `nexus.js` and content scripts using `postMessage` and unified chrome message listeners.

### ⚠️ Current Blockers for the Smarter Model:
*   **Syntax Normalization**: The IDE occasionally flags valid template literals as JSX or "Invalid Characters" due to parser conflicts.
*   **DOM Injection Overlap**: Managing the z-index and placement when other extensions (like Multiselect) are active.
*   **Background Lifecycle**: Ensuring the service worker doesn't go dormant during long video hoarding sessions.
